subject: [PATCH v5 9/9] mm: switch deferred split shrinker to list_lru

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary:
Reviewer argued that capturing sub-costly-order slab allocation failures in an event counter is unnecessary, as such failures are rare and would be logged to dmesg instead, and suggested removing the count_mthp_stat() call or replacing it with a goto next instruction.

Reviewer noted that the fallback logic in the deferred split shrinker may not be necessary because the size of the list_lru allocation remains unchanged for lower orders until order 0, and suggested leaving the existing code as is.
reviews.raw_body:
This isn't an allocation we expect to fail with any sort of routine
that we'd need to capture it in the event counter. It would warn in
dmesg if it did. But in practice it can't happen at all, since it's a
sub-costly-order slab allocation and the allocator would loop and OOM
kill stuff until it succeeds.

---

Again I don't think it matters, but fallback seems a bit more correct
because the size of the list_lru allocation doesn't change with lower
orders (until we hit 0).

So I think we can just leave it as is.

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch updates the deferred split shrinker to use the list_lru data structure, improving its performance and efficiency.
